paper_title: Sediment Sample Analysis from Raja Ampat Archipelago

methods.description: Process for collecting sediment samples from the Raja Ampat Archipelago for further analysis.

steps.description: The samples were collected by snorkeling and SCUBA diving in September 2011 by M Langer.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples using snorkeling and SCUBA diving

steps.description: Sediment surface samples from the top 2 cm were collected from the fore-reef slope of fringing reefs, with two samples from a patch reef, and two samples from a sandy channel with sparse coral cover.
steps.searchable_summary: collect top 2 cm sediment samples from specified reef locations

methods.description: Preparation of sediment samples for imaging and analysis.

steps.description: All samples were washed through a 63 µm sieve and dried at 50°C in an oven overnight.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and dry sediment samples using a 63 µm sieve and oven

methods.description: Imaging of foraminifera specimens using scanning electron microscopy.

steps.description: Foraminifera were picked from each sample and the best preserved specimens were imaged using a Tescan VEGA MV2300 Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM) at the Steinmann Institute of the University of Bonn.
Digital plates were assembled using Adobe Photoshop CS6.

methods.description: Imaging of samples using micro-computer tomography for detailed visualization.

steps.description: Micro-computer tomography (CT) scan imaging was conducted using a phoenix v|tome|x s computed tomography system at the Steinmann Institute.
Visualization was carried out with Avizo 7.1.0.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize CT scan images using Avizo
